title: feat: OAuth 2.1 authorization-code grant with PKCE + RFC 8414 discovery
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `POST /api/oauth/token`: form-encoded, `authorization_code` grant with mandatory PKCE S256, single-use KV-backed codes, RFC 6749 error objects; also accepts the `device_code` grant via the shared device-flow exchange
- Adds `POST /api/oauth/code` (consent-side code creation, re-validates pk_ client_id + redirectUris allowlist) and `GET /api/oauth/userinfo`
- Adds `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` (RFC 8414); routes `/.well-known/*` to the worker via `run_worker_first`
- Consent page: `response_type=code` switches the callback from the legacy `#api_key=` fragment to `?code=...&state=...`; explicit S256 + well-formed challenge required before key minting
- Legacy fragment flow and `/api/device/*` unchanged; pk_ keys stay the client_id
- Adds `apps/oauth-client-demo/`: zero-dep reference client (discovery → PKCE → exchange → userinfo → delegated generation), smoke-tested end-to-end
- 17 new tests; device + authorize-config suites pass

Addresses #8585
Addresses #11369

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Keys to the garden
gist.blurb: We've added full OAuth 2.1 support so your apps can securely request access to the hive. No more passing API keys around like loose pollen!
gist.summary: Added OAuth 2.1 authorization-code grant with PKCE and RFC 8414 discovery to enter.pollinations.ai, enabling secure, delegated API access.
gist.impact: Developers can now use standard OAuth flows via /api/oauth/token to authenticate users without handling raw API keys, complete with a new zero-dependency reference client.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a friendly gatekeeper, securely handing a glowing digital key to the Monitor Robot. Represents secure authorization and OAuth handshakes.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny security guard hat, handing a glowing golden key to the chunky Monitor Robot. The robot's CRT screen displays a bright green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
